title: Why fight the angels
wikitext:
[]: Why fight the angels?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Aug 09, 2014 2:35 am The search engine actually didn't help me on this, and I was surprised. It seemed like a simple enough question. Anyway, the goal of SEELE was to initiate Third Impact, which would lead to human instrumentality. Third Impact is caused by an angel making contact with Adam. So.. why fight the angels? Why not just have any one of them (the first one that we see attacking, I guess) make contact with Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Aug 09, 2014 2:49 am Because Seele wanted 3I on their own terms, and not those of an Angel.

[]: Re: Why fight the angels?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Aug 09, 2014 2:49 am Tumbling Down wrote: The search engine actually didn't help me on this, and I was surprised. It seemed like a simple enough question. Anyway, the goal of SEELE was to initiate Third Impact, which would lead to human instrumentality. Third Impact is caused by an angel making contact with Adam. So.. why fight the angels? Why not just have any one of them (the first one that we see attacking, I guess) make contact with Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 09, 2014 5:18 am Depending one what goes into the mix, who is calling the shots and what they want to happen there are potentially all sorts of impacts. Seele didn't just want any old impact, they were trying to achieve a specific result.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Aug 09, 2014 1:15 pm Thanks! That makes it clearer.

Article: Why fight the angels
[]: Why fight the angels?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Aug 09, 2014 2:35 am The search engine actually didn't help me on this, and I was surprised. It seemed like a simple enough question. Anyway, the goal of SEELE was to initiate Third Impact, which would lead to human instrumentality. Third Impact is caused by an angel making contact with Adam. So.. why fight the angels? Why not just have any one of them (the first one that we see attacking, I guess) make contact with Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Aug 09, 2014 2:49 am Because Seele wanted 3I on their own terms, and not those of an Angel.

[]: Re: Why fight the angels?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Aug 09, 2014 2:49 am Tumbling Down wrote: The search engine actually didn't help me on this, and I was surprised. It seemed like a simple enough question. Anyway, the goal of SEELE was to initiate Third Impact, which would lead to human instrumentality. Third Impact is caused by an angel making contact with Adam. So.. why fight the angels? Why not just have any one of them (the first one that we see attacking, I guess) make contact with Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 09, 2014 5:18 am Depending one what goes into the mix, who is calling the shots and what they want to happen there are potentially all sorts of impacts. Seele didn't just want any old impact, they were trying to achieve a specific result.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Aug 09, 2014 1:15 pm Thanks! That makes it clearer.
